# Routing And Controllers

Sources used: Context7 `/websites/laravel_13_x` results for Laravel 13.x routing, controllers, resource controllers, controller route groups, and middleware on resource routes.

## Application Routing Bootstrap
The docs show default routing configured in `bootstrap/app.php`:

```php
<?php

use Illuminate\Foundation\Application;

return Application::configure(basePath: dirname(__DIR__))
    ->withRouting(
        web: __DIR__.'/../routes/web.php',
        commands: __DIR__.'/../routes/console.php',
        health: '/up',
    )->create();
```

Confirmed values: `bootstrap/app.php`, `routes/web.php`, `routes/console.php`, `/up`, `Illuminate\Foundation\Application`, `Application::configure(...)`, `withRouting(...)`, and `create()`.

## Basic Routes
The docs show `Route::get()` with a closure and a controller action:

```php
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Route;
use App\Http\Controllers\UserController;

Route::get('/greeting', function () {
    return 'Hello World';
});

Route::get('/user', [UserController::class, 'index']);
```

The docs describe these routes as typically placed in `routes/web.php` for web requests.

## Basic Controller Generation
The docs confirm:

```shell
php artisan make:controller UserController
```

The generated controller is created in `app/Http/Controllers` by default.

## Middleware Generation
The docs confirm:

```shell
php artisan make:middleware EnsureTokenIsValid
```

The generated middleware is created in `app/Http/Middleware`.

## Resource Controllers
The docs confirm:

```shell
php artisan make:controller PhotoController --resource
```

The command generates a controller at `app/Http/Controllers/PhotoController.php`. The docs say the controller contains a method for each available resource operation.

The docs confirm registering resource routes with:

```php
Route::resource('photos', PhotoController::class);
```

The docs also mention `routes/web.php` and `routes/api.php` as files where `Route::resource` can define resource routes.

## Resource Controller Actions
The documented resource route table is:

| Verb | URI | Action | Route Name |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GET | `/photos` | `index` | `photos.index` |
| GET | `/photos/create` | `create` | `photos.create` |
| POST | `/photos` | `store` | `photos.store` |
| GET | `/photos/{photo}` | `show` | `photos.show` |
| GET | `/photos/{photo}/edit` | `edit` | `photos.edit` |
| PUT/PATCH | `/photos/{photo}` | `update` | `photos.update` |
| DELETE | `/photos/{photo}` | `destroy` | `photos.destroy` |

The docs state `route:list` gives a quick overview of application routes.

## Controller Route Groups
The docs show grouping routes by controller:

```php
use App\Http\Controllers\OrderController;

Route::controller(OrderController::class)->group(function () {
    Route::get('/orders/{id}', 'show');
    Route::post('/orders', 'store');
});
```

## Middleware On Resource Routes
The docs show middleware applied to all routes generated by a resource or singleton resource controller:

```php
Route::resource('users', UserController::class)
    ->middleware(['auth', 'verified']);

Route::singleton('profile', ProfileController::class)
    ->middleware('auth');
```

The docs show method-specific middleware with `middlewareFor(...)`:

```php
Route::resource('users', UserController::class)
    ->middlewareFor('show', 'auth');

Route::apiResource('users', UserController::class)
    ->middlewareFor(['show', 'update'], 'auth');

Route::singleton('profile', ProfileController::class)
    ->middlewareFor('show', 'auth');
```

Confirmed route builder methods from these snippets: `middleware(...)`, `middlewareFor(...)`, `Route::resource(...)`, `Route::apiResource(...)`, and `Route::singleton(...)`.

## Topics Requiring Fresh Lookup
Run a new Context7 query before using route parameters beyond shown examples, route model binding, named route URL generation, route groups beyond `Route::controller(...)`, redirects, fallback routes, route caching, API route bootstrapping, controller dependency injection, request objects, response helpers, singleton resource details beyond shown middleware examples, or nested resource routes.
